question_text: A student builds two electromagnets using identical iron cores and the same length of copper wire. Electromagnet A has the wire wrapped around the core 50 times and is connected to a 6V battery. Electromagnet B has the wire wrapped around the core 100 times and is connected to a 3V battery. Which electromagnet will be stronger, and explain your reasoning by considering both the number of turns and the voltage applied.
correct_answer: Electromagnet B will be stronger. Although it has half the voltage (3V vs 6V), it has twice as many turns of wire (100 vs 50). The number of turns has a greater effect on electromagnet strength than voltage. More turns create a stronger magnetic field because the magnetic effects of each loop add together.
explanation: Electromagnet strength depends on both the number of wire turns and the current flowing through the wire. While Electromagnet A has higher voltage, Electromagnet B's double number of turns significantly increases the strength of the magnetic field, and this effect outweighs the lower voltage.
